title: The Present Subjunctive, Deepened
description: Move from the A2 first contact to the full present subjunctive: how every form is built from the yo-form, the spelling changes, the stem-changing verbs and all the truly irregular verbs (sea, vaya, haya, sepa, dé, esté). The triggers come in Lesson 2.

cards.back: Build the subjunctive from the yo-form: drop -o, add the opposite endings
cards.notes: The golden rule: take the **yo** form of the present indicative (*hablo*, *como*, *digo*), drop the **-o**, and add the *opposite* vowel endings. This keeps almost every irregularity automatically (*digo → diga*).

cards.front: hable, hables, hable, hablemos, habléis, hablen
cards.notes: -AR verbs take **e-endings**: -e, -es, -e, -emos, -éis, -en. The vowel *flips* from the indicative's -a.

cards.front: coma, comas, coma, comamos, comáis, coman
cards.notes: -ER and -IR verbs take **a-endings**: -a, -as, -a, -amos, -áis, -an. So *comer → coma*, *vivir → viva*. The vowel flips from -e/-i to -a.

cards.front: tener → tengo → tenga; decir → digo → diga
cards.back: verbs irregular in the yo-form stay irregular in the subjunctive
cards.notes: Because the rule starts from the yo-form, verbs like *tener (tengo), decir (digo), hacer (hago), salir (salgo), poner (pongo), conocer (conozco)* carry their irregular stem through every person: *tenga, tengas...*

cards.front: buscar → busque; llegar → llegue; empezar → empiece
cards.notes: Unlike the preterite (only *yo*), here the spelling change runs through **all** persons: *busque, busques, busque...*; *llegue...*; and *-zar* → *-ce* (*empiece*).

cards.back: -AR/-ER stem-changers: change in all forms EXCEPT nosotros/vosotros
cards.notes: *pensar (e→ie)* → piense, pienses, piense, **pensemos**, **penséis**, piensen. Same boot pattern as the indicative: nosotros/vosotros keep the plain stem.
-IR verbs add an extra change in *nosotros/vosotros*: *dormir* → duerma... but **durmamos, durmáis**; *pedir* → pida... **pidamos**. *sentir* → sienta... **sintamos**.

cards.front: ser→sea, ir→vaya, haber→haya, saber→sepa, dar→dé, estar→esté
cards.notes: These don't come from the yo-form and must be memorised: *ser → sea*, *ir → vaya*, *haber → haya*, *saber → sepa*, *dar → dé* (with accent, vs. *de*), *estar → esté* (accents on esté, estés, estén).

cards.front: haya, hayas, haya, hayamos, hayáis, hayan
cards.back: present subjunctive of haber (for the perfect subjunctive)
cards.notes: *haya* + past participle = the perfect subjunctive: *Espero que **hayas comido*** (I hope you have eaten). Don't confuse *haya* (subjunctive) with *allá* (over there) or *halla* (he/she finds).

cards.front: Creo que viene. / No creo que venga.
cards.back: indicative for fact/assertion, subjunctive for the unreal/subjective
cards.notes: The subjunctive is a **mood**, not a tense. Roughly: assert a fact → indicative (*Creo que viene*); express doubt, wish, emotion or denial → subjunctive (*No creo que venga*). The triggers come in Lesson 2.

cards.front: I want you to study. → Quiero que estudies.
cards.back: English has no living subjunctive — watch the 'que + change of subject' pattern
cards.notes: English uses an infinitive (*I want you to study*); Spanish needs **que + subjunctive** when the subject changes: *Quiero que estudies*. With no subject change, use the infinitive: *Quiero estudiar*.

*dé* keeps an accent to distinguish it from the preposition *de*. *estar* takes accents on **esté, estés, estén** (but not *estemos, estéis*… actually *estéis* has one). Accents matter for meaning and for the validator.

steps.title: From first contact to full command
steps.body:
# The Present Subjunctive, deepened

In A2 you met the subjunctive as a mood for **wishes, emotions, doubt and the unreal**. Now we build *every* form reliably.

## The golden rule: start from the yo-form

1. Take the **yo** form of the present indicative: *hablar → hablo*, *comer → como*, *decir → digo*.
2. Drop the **-o**.
3. Add the **opposite** endings:

| | -AR (*hablar*) | -ER/-IR (*comer / vivir*) |
|---|---|---|
| yo | hable | coma |
| tú | hables | comas |
| él/ella | hable | coma |
| nosotros | hablemos | comamos |
| vosotros | habléis | comáis |
| ellos | hablen | coman |

The vowel **flips**: -AR verbs take *e*, -ER/-IR verbs take *a*.

> Because we start from *yo*, irregular yo-forms come along for free: *tener (tengo) → tenga*, *decir (digo) → diga*, *conocer (conozco) → conozca*.

steps.title: Spelling changes and stem-changers
steps.body:
## Spelling changes — in every person

To keep the sound, **-car, -gar, -zar** verbs change spelling throughout (not just *yo*):

- *buscar* → **busque, busques, busque, busquemos, busquéis, busquen**
- *llegar* → **llegue...**  ·  *empezar* → **empiece...**

## Stem-changing verbs

**-AR and -ER** stem-changers follow the boot: they change in all forms **except** *nosotros/vosotros*.

- *pensar (e→ie)* → piense, pienses, piense, **pensemos, penséis**, piensen
- *poder (o→ue)* → pueda... **podamos, podáis**... puedan

**-IR** stem-changers change there **too**, with a special vowel:

- *dormir* → duerma, duermas... **durmamos, durmáis**... duerman (o→u)
- *pedir* → pida... **pidamos, pidáis**... pidan (e→i)
- *sentir* → sienta... **sintamos, sintáis**... sientan (e→i)

steps.title: The six irregulars and the English gap
steps.body:
## Six verbs you simply memorise

These don't come from the yo-form:

| Verb | Subjunctive |
|---|---|
| ser | **sea**, seas, sea, seamos, seáis, sean |
| ir | **vaya**, vayas, vaya, vayamos, vayáis, vayan |
| haber | **haya**, hayas, haya, hayamos, hayáis, hayan |
| saber | **sepa**, sepas... |
| dar | **dé**, des, dé, demos, deis, den |
| estar | **esté**, estés, esté, estemos, estéis, estén |

Note the accents: *dé* (vs. the preposition *de*) and *esté/estés/esté/estén*.

## Mind the English gap

Modern English barely has a subjunctive, so the structure feels alien. The key trigger is **que + a change of subject**:

- *I want **to** study* → *Quiero estudiar* (no change → infinitive)
- *I want **you to** study* → *Quiero que **estudies*** (change → que + subjunctive)

Never translate *to* literally here. The full list of triggers is Lesson 2.

exercise.prompt: Match each verb with its correct yo present-subjunctive form.
Translate: *I want you to study more.* (change of subject → que + subjunctive)
Translate: *I hope (that) you are happy.* (ser → subjunctive)
Which sentence correctly says 'I don't think he will come'? (doubt → subjunctive)
exercise.hint: no creer que expresses doubt → subjunctive; venir (vengo) → venga.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the present subjunctive of *buscar* for nosotros.
exercise.sentence: El jefe quiere que ___ una solución entre todos.
exercise.hint: buscar → busqu- in every person; nosotros ending -emos → busquemos.

steps.title: Build: I hope it doesn't rain

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow.* (Ojalá + subjunctive)
exercise.hint: Ojalá always takes the subjunctive; llover → llueva.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the present subjunctive of *dormir* for nosotros.
exercise.sentence: Es importante que ___ ocho horas cada noche.
exercise.hint: -IR stem-changers shift o→u in nosotros too: durmamos.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *The doctor recommends that you rest.* (recomendar que + subjunctive, usted)
accept: El médico recomienda que usted descanse.
distractors: El médico recomienda que descanses usted.

exercise.prompt: Match each infinitive with its (irregular) present-subjunctive yo-form.
Translate: *I'm glad (that) you know the truth.* (saber → subjunctive)
accept: Me alegro de que sepas la verdad.
me alegro de que sepas la verdad
Me alegro de que sepas la verdad.
Me alegro que sepas la verdad.
exercise.hint: alegrarse de que + subjunctive; saber → sepas (tú).
distractors: Me alegro de que sabes la verdad.
Me alegro de que conozcas la verdad.
Estoy contento de que sabrás la verdad.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the subjunctive of *haber* (tú) to form the perfect subjunctive.
exercise.sentence: Espero que ___ terminado el trabajo antes de la reunión.
exercise.hint: haber (tú) subjunctive = hayas; + past participle = perfect subjunctive.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *It's important that we be on time.* (estar → subjunctive, nosotros)
accept: Es importante que estemos a tiempo.
es importante que estemos a tiempo
Es importante que estemos a tiempo.
exercise.hint: Es importante que + subjunctive; estar → estemos (nosotros).
distractors: Es importante que estamos a tiempo.
Es importante que seamos a tiempo.
Es importante estar a tiempo nosotros.
